statement.p: If you double a certain positive number, then also add {{\frac{15}{2}}} to that same number, and multiply the results, you get {8}\text{.} What was the original number?

solution.p: Let x be the mystery number. We are told that (2x)\left(x+{{\frac{15}{2}}}\right)={8}\text{.} So
Now we can apply the quadratic formula:
Both -8 and {{\frac{1}{2}}} solve the equation, but we were asked to find a positive number. So the mystery number is {{\frac{1}{2}}}\text{.}
